diagnosis:
The williams_cci strategy is a short-duration mean-reversion system that fires when Williams %R and CCI are simultaneously deep in oversold territory (long signal) or overbought territory (short signal). It targets a very narrow setup: low-ADX (max 35) conditions indicating weak trend, a BB squeeze (bandwidth percentile below 30), and a volume spike (volume_ratio >= 1.76). Entry lag is 1 bar (correct). Stops and take-profits are ATR-scaled. Time stop is 24 hours.

The strategy currently emits both "long" and "short" signals. The short branch fires when WR >= -21.7 and CCI >= 148.9 simultaneously, which is the overbought-reversal condition. In Phase-3 spot-long-only the short branch must be dropped entirely — not converted to a short. This eliminates roughly half of all potential signal events.

The evolved PARAMS are highly specific (wr_oversold=-89.53, cci_long_threshold=-92.62, stop_loss_atr_mult=1.32, take_profit_atr_mult=2.63) and were produced by ~1,061 genetic evaluations that yielded only 19 trades in-sample and a walk-forward Sharpe of 1.50. The strategy was pruned from the STRATEGY_REGISTRY in Round 2 for sub-1.5% positive fitness rate across the 36K-eval evolution run. It is NOT currently in the active STRATEGY_REGISTRY.

Edge/Weakness: The long-side edge hypothesis is that an extreme dual-oscillator oversold reading during a low-volatility (BB squeeze) period with a simultaneous volume spike marks a local capitulation bottom that tends to revert quickly within 24 hours. The weakness is extreme filter stacking — the conjunction of four Boolean conditions (ADX < 35, volume_ratio > 1.76, bb_bw_percentile < 30, WR <= -89.5, CCI <= -92.6) produces very few bars per year, causing sample fragility. 19 trades over an evolution window signals severe under-trading. The evolved thresholds are suspiciously precise (four significant figures), consistent with overfitting to a short in-sample window.
long_only_hypothesis: In spot crypto markets, extreme dual-oscillator oversold conditions (Williams %R near -100 AND CCI near -100) occurring within a Bollinger Band squeeze (low relative bandwidth) and coinciding with an above-average volume spike identify high-probability short-term capitulation events. Buyers step in aggressively during the volume-spike bar while the asset is already deeply compressed. The spot long-only edge is: buy the capitulation, hold for a mean-reversion bounce toward the BB midline within 24-48 hours, and exit via ATR-scaled take-profit or time stop. The long-or-cash framing is strict: when the overbought condition fires, do nothing (stay in cash) rather than shorting. A secondary long-only filter: only enter long when close is above the BMSB (20-SMA / 21-EMA band), ensuring the capitulation is a pullback within a macro bull structure rather than a continuation of a bear. RSI_7 < 35 at entry adds a fast confirming oversold filter. OBV divergence (rising OBV despite falling price) can further gate entries where smart money accumulation is visible.
market_techniques: Mean-reversion with capitulation trigger: dual WR+CCI extreme oversold as entry catalyst
Volatility regime filtering: BB squeeze percentile gates entries to low-volatility compression phases where post-spike rebounds are sharper
Volume-spike confirmation: above-average volume on the signal bar confirms genuine panic selling rather than slow drift
ATR-normalized stop/target placement: SL below recent low adjusted by ATR multiple, TP toward BB midline or fixed ATR multiple
Macro trend filter via BMSB: only take long entries when price is above the 20-SMA/21-EMA support band to avoid catching falling knives in bear markets
Time stop discipline: 24-48h maximum hold avoids position decay in mean-reversion that fails to snap back
Regime gate: restrict entries to lateral and mean_reversion regimes (exclude strong-trend regimes where mean-reversion fails systematically)
Volatility targeting for position sizing: scale notional inversely to realized GK vol to maintain constant risk per trade rather than fixed-fraction sizing
useful_features: trend: adx_14 — low-ADX filter ensuring mean-reversion regime
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — BB bandwidth squeeze gate (compression precedes expansion)
volume: volume_ratio — volume spike confirmation of capitulation bar
volume: volume_zscore — z-score normalizes volume spike across volatility regimes
volume: obv_divergence — OBV rising while price falling signals accumulation
momentum: rsi_7 — fast RSI oversold confirmation below 35
trend: bmsb_bullish — macro bull-market support band filter for long-only gate
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass vol for volatility targeting and regime detection
price: dist_from_high_20 — distance from 20-bar high; deep negative values confirm oversold
price: bb_pct_b — position within BB bands; low pct_b (near lower band) confirms oversold
microstructure: buying_pressure — intrabar buying pressure on signal candle (should be recovering)
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — pressure_imbalance turning positive on signal bar
regime: regime label (lateral / mean_reversion) — categorical regime gate

genome_params.name: wr_period
genome_params.note: Williams %R lookback; shorter = more reactive, longer = smoother

genome_params.name: wr_oversold
genome_params.note: WR entry threshold for long; evolved value -89.5 is a reasonable interior

genome_params.name: cci_period
genome_params.note: CCI lookback; 14-20 is the standard range

genome_params.name: cci_long_threshold
genome_params.note: CCI must be below this for long entry; allow wider range than evolved -92.6

genome_params.name: bb_squeeze_percentile
genome_params.note: Max BB bandwidth percentile allowed; lower = stricter compression requirement

genome_params.name: adx_max
genome_params.note: Max ADX allowed; above this the market is trending and mean-reversion fails

genome_params.name: volume_spike_threshold
genome_params.note: Min volume_ratio required; too high = too few trades

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for stop below entry; tight SL needed for short holds

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take-profit above entry; must exceed SL

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Max trade duration; mean-reversion either works quickly or fails

genome_params.name: require_bb_squeeze
genome_params.note: Toggle BB squeeze filter on/off; allows evolution to test without it

genome_params.name: rsi_oversold_max
genome_params.note: Max RSI_7 at entry; adds fast oscillator confirmation

genome_params.name: require_bmsb_bullish
genome_params.note: Toggle macro BMSB filter; when True only enters in bull-market structure

do_not_optimize: wr_overbought (entire short branch is dropped in Phase-3; this param is unused)
allowed_regimes (hardcode to [lateral, mean_reversion]; fixing avoids regime label snooping)
require_trend_regime (keep True; toggling this off removes a principled safeguard against trend-following regime contamination)

risk_management.stop: ATR-based hard stop: entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14. Evolved mult = 1.32 ATR is tight and appropriate for a 24h mean-reversion trade; allow sweep 0.8-2.5x. Stop is intrabar-checked (low <= stop_loss triggers exit at stop price per backtester logic).
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-based TP: entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14. Evolved 2.63x ATR gives RR ~2:1 vs 1.32x SL. Sweep range 1.5-4.0x. Alternatively target the BB midline (bb_middle column) as a dynamic TP anchor.
risk_management.trailing: No trailing stop recommended for this strategy — the mean-reversion bounce is expected to reach TP within 24h or fail. Adding a trailing stop introduces extra parameter complexity that likely overfits given the thin trade count.
risk_management.vol_target: Apply Garman-Klass volatility targeting for position sizing: scale notional so 1-ATR move = 1% of equity. Cap at max_position_pct. This automatically reduces size in high-vol regimes (bear markets, flash crashes) where mean-reversion fails most often.
risk_management.max_size: Cap at 20% of equity per trade (BacktestConfig.max_position_pct = 0.20). With vol-targeting active the realized size will typically be smaller than the cap in normal market conditions.
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 12-hour cooldown after any trade exit before the next entry is considered. This prevents re-entry into a continuing slide and reduces turnover-driven cost drag.
risk_management.risk_off: Gate all long entries on bmsb_bullish = True (price above both 20-SMA and 21-EMA). When bmsb_bullish is False the market is in a structurally bearish posture and mean-reversion longs frequently become knife-catches. Additionally, if ADX > adx_max the trend is too strong for mean-reversion — this filter is already in the code.

metrics: Sharpe ratio (target >= 1.0 WF-OOS; evolved IS Sharpe 1.59 must survive OOS)
Sortino ratio (downside-only denominator; especially relevant for a strategy that should avoid sharp drawdowns)
Max drawdown % (full-sample peak-to-trough; ruin gate: equity never <= 0)
Hit rate (% winning trades; mean-reversion should achieve > 55%; evolved strategy had 19 trades — too thin)
Number of trades (minimum 30 per year to be statistically meaningful; evolved 19 total is a red flag)
Average trade PnL net of all costs (commission + slippage; must be positive after 2x slippage stress test)
Turnover / exposure fraction (% of time in position; low expected for this strategy)
Cost sensitivity test: double slippage_bps and re-run — alpha should survive given ATR-based TP is wide enough
Walk-forward OOS Sharpe vs IS Sharpe degradation (target OOS degradation < 30%)
Per-year Sharpe stability across 2019-2023 (pre-lockbox): no single year should drive all returns
Bootstrap / Monte Carlo ruin probability (target prob_ruin < 5% across 1000 simulations)
Profit factor (gross wins / gross losses; target > 1.3 OOS)
DSR (Deflated Sharpe Ratio deflated by cumulative trial count; must pass dsr_min >= 0.95)
sweep_changes: Re-add williams_cci to STRATEGY_REGISTRY with the revised long-only param_space (remove wr_overbought and cci_short_threshold entirely; add rsi_oversold_max and require_bmsb_bullish)
Remove the short branch from williams_cci_strategy: delete the elif block (lines 117-118) so only side='long' is ever returned; if side is None after the long check, return None
Add RSI_7 oversold filter: require current['rsi_7'] <= rsi_oversold_max (sweep 25-45) as an additional confirmation gate
Add BMSB macro filter: when require_bmsb_bullish=True, return None if current['bmsb_bullish'] is False or NaN
Widen param ranges for sweep: wr_period 7-20, cci_period 10-25, cci_long_threshold -150 to -50, volume_spike_threshold 1.2-3.0, bb_squeeze_percentile 15-50
Change strength calculation: remove the 'else' (short) branch from strength computation; compute strength purely from long-side WR depth and volume ratio
Update stop/TP construction: remove short-side stop_loss/take_profit lines; only long-side SL/TP needed
Add BMSB feature columns to the bar read (bmsb_bullish is already produced by build_all_features)
Set min_trades target >= 50 in fitness evaluation (raise from the 19-trade evolved baseline) via low_trade_penalty in fitness function; current threshold is 30 — increase to 40 for this strategy
Run pre-lockbox walk-forward on 2019-01-01 to 2024-06-07 only; lockbox 2024-06-07 to 2026-06-07 remains sealed throughout all sweeps
methodological_risks: Extreme filter stacking: the conjunction of five simultaneous conditions (ADX, volume, BB squeeze, WR extreme, CCI extreme) produces very few bars — 19 trades in the evolved version is statistically negligible and cannot distinguish edge from noise

Evolved threshold precision (e.g., wr_oversold=-89.5301 to 4 decimal places) is a strong overfitting signal; these values should be treated as starting points for a wider sweep, not as discovered truth

Williams %R is computed fresh bar-by-bar using raw numpy arrays in _williams_r() — this is correct but creates a subtle O(n*period) bottleneck at scale; not a leakage risk but a speed concern for large sweeps

CCI computed identically bar-by-bar (_cci function) — no leakage, but the strategy does not use the precomputed feature pipeline columns (bb_bw_percentile uses 'bb_bw_percentile' but WR/CCI are recomputed inline); this inconsistency means the genome's wr_period and cci_period diverge from any pipeline-cached values

bb_bw_percentile uses a 100-bar rolling window; the WalkForwardValidator embargo must remain >= 120 bars to prevent the percentile feature from bridging train/test windows — this is already handled in the engine (embargo_bars=120) but any future feature additions must respect this constraint

Regime label leakage risk: the _regime column used by the strategy gate (regime not in allowed_regimes) must be computed without future data; verify the regime classifier uses only past bars and that the same regime column is reproduced identically in OOS evaluation

Short removal impact: dropping the short branch reduces signal frequency by approximately half on any given asset, further worsening the thin-trade problem; the long-only version will likely produce even fewer signals than the 19 trades observed in evolution

Bitcoin/crypto bull-bias survivorship: mean-reversion strategies in crypto 2019-2023 benefited from a structurally upward trending market; OOS 2024-2026 includes a more mature market cycle and different vol regime; the BMSB filter partially mitigates this but does not eliminate it

Cross-asset generalization: the strategy was penalized in Round 2 for working on XRP but not generalizing across assets (cross_asset_xrp_only_penalty); in the long-only version this risk persists — add cross-asset sweep across BTC, ETH, SOL during in-sample calibration

Monte Carlo ruin risk: with only ~19 trades and a tight 24-hour time stop, a single streak of 5-6 consecutive losses at 1.32x ATR SL can cause a meaningful equity drawdown; vol-targeting sizing is essential to prevent compounding of consecutive losers
